title: Guide 2 – Case Study: Angkor and The Tourism Development Strategic Plan 2012–2020
highlights:
Angkor represents some of the key threats and concerns World Heritage sites across the world may also have to deal with and manage. When Angkor was first inscribed in 1992, it was immediately placed on the List of World Heritage in Danger due to threat from conflict between Cambodia and Vietnam. It was only removed from this List  ...    ## What did they do?
**‘Managing heritage at Angkor requires managing tourism’ (Tourism Management Plan 2012-2020)** The involved parties recognized that unless management dramatically changed to meet the contemporary needs of Angkor and its population, the site would be damaged beyond recovery. It has also been recognized that tourism represents both  ...    destination and a place for people to live.
